methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $65M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $200M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: Graphyte
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M

private.name: Fourth Power
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $150M

private.name: Steady Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $110M

data.narrative:
Peak Energy, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Landon Mossburg and Liam O'Connor, has raised $65M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of three same-stage, same-sector private companies (Graphyte at $200M, Fourth Power at $150M, and Steady Energy at $110M), alongside one public comparable, with additional reference to a broader universe of eight private climate-tech firms including TAE Technologies and Redwood Materials.

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis, using the $65M total raised as a proxy base value and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A advancement, implies a $130M valuation. Peer-median analysis across the three direct comparables yields a $150M midpoint, with a range spanning $110M to $200M. These methods converge closely, with the stage-progression result falling within the peer distribution.

Synthesis of both methods supports a valuation range of $110M to $172M, with a median of $140M. The relatively tight clustering between stage-progression ($130M) and peer-median ($150M) lends confidence to this band. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the three peer valuations—which span a material $90M range—and whether Peak Energy's capital efficiency and unit economics align with the high or low quartile of this peer set. The single public comparable provides limited additional anchor.
